item.title: What I Learned About Masculinity at Thai Kickboxing School
item.description: A week inside a grueling Muay Thai training program in Phuket.

item.title: The Native Leader Who Tied His People’s Fate to the Patriot Cause
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Radical Pacifist Who Inspired the First Armed Rebellion of the Founding Era
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Teenage Soldier Who Revealed How the Patriots Really Won the War
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Patriot Housewife Whose Plays Helped Push America Toward Revolution
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Man of Faith Who Heard a Righteous Call in the Founding Credo
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Enslaved Woman Who Sued for Freedom and Emancipated Herself
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Itinerant Preacher Who Helped Secure the Separation of Church and State
item.description: For the 250th anniversary, The Times Magazine asked leading historians to profile founding-era Americans whose roles in the drama have been often overlooked.

item.title: The Many Founders of the United States
item.description: In the past 50 years, the way we tell the story of the Revolution has become dramatically more complex. Can it still inspire us all?

item.title: My Awful Mother Is Getting Alzheimer’s. What Do I Owe Her?
item.description: The thought of having her live with me makes me physically sick.

item.title: Robby Hoffman Will Always Feel Poor, No Matter How Rich She Gets
item.description: The comedian and actor says class and the way she grew up inform everything about the way she lives now.

item.title: Did American-Style ‘Gentle Parenting’ Spoil French Children?
item.description: As positive parenting takes over France, one psychologist’s call for a return to discipline has set off a furious debate.

item.title: Why Is Florida Executing So Many Prisoners?
item.description: In most of the country, executions are a thing of the past. But one state has been carrying them out at a record pace.

item.title: How A.I. Might Change the Way Doctors Think
item.description: For generations, writing up a summary of a patient exam was a vital step for physicians trying to make an accurate diagnosis. What happens when A.I. does it for them?
